title: Sequence number wasted on QueueFullError in link_layer.send()
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:247-282

The send() method calls _next_seqnum() to consume a sequence number BEFORE attempting to queue the frame. If tx_queue.push() raises QueueFullError, the sequence number has been consumed but the packet was never queued or transmitted.

This creates gaps in the sequence number space. While replay protection handles gaps gracefully, this wastes sequence numbers unnecessarily and accelerates epoch exhaustion.

Fix: Move sequence number allocation to after successful queue push, or only increment on successful push.
close_reason: send() now peeks seqnum before push; consumes only on success
